presentation.chiefComplaint: Fever, confusion, and weakness for two days

history.hpi: Two days of fevers, rigors, dysuria and worsening confusion. Family notes decreased oral intake and reduced urine output. Today he is lethargic and not himself.
redFlags: Sepsis criteria: fever + tachycardia + tachypnea + hypotension
Altered mental status (acute confusion from baseline)
Requires early antibiotics and fluids (sepsis bundle)

expert.esiRationale: Septic shock physiology (hypotension, tachycardia, tachypnea, altered mental status) in an elderly patient. High-risk, time-critical for the sepsis bundle but not yet requiring an immediate door-side life-saving procedure such as intubation, so ESI 2. Under-triage here delays antibiotics and fluids.

provenance.sourceRef: ED Triage Trainer hand-authored seed case
